slice_name: Create and manage client projects with currency and billable rate

touched_contracts.shape: Client Project and related entity records (E2 — Client Project, E5 — Currency designation, E6 — Client record, E7 — Billable Rate) — persisted and retrieved by Project Management to/from the Data Store. Access covers create, read (list + detail), update, and delete of project-level records. Field-level schemas for E2/E5/E6/E7 deferred to S4 per cut deferred[].
failure_modes: store-unavailable — PostgreSQL instance unreachable; project create/read/update/delete operations fail
constraint-violation — FK integrity failure (e.g. orphaned project referencing deleted freelancer) or uniqueness violation on project name within owner scope
not-found — queried project record does not exist in the store (deleted or wrong owner scope)

touched_contracts.shape: Authenticated freelancer session — Project Management calls Identity & Auth in-process to resolve the current session and obtain the freelancer identity (E1 reference) used to scope all project data reads and writes to the owning freelancer. No field-level session token design invented here; shape is the session presence signal and owner identity reference.
failure_modes: no-valid-session — session absent or expired; Identity & Auth returns no authenticated identity; Project Management must reject the request (unauthorized)
callee-error — Identity & Auth raises an exception during session resolution (e.g. store lookup failure for identity record); Project Management cannot determine ownership and must fail the request

touched_contracts.shape: Project management page request dispatch — Web Ingress routes an authenticated HTTP request in-process to Project Management, which returns a server-rendered HTML response (project list, project detail, project create/edit/delete forms). No wire format or URL path schema invented here; shape is the request/response dispatch at the MPA routing seam.
failure_modes: callee-error — Project Management raises an unhandled exception during request processing; Web Ingress must return an appropriate HTTP error response
not-found — requested project resource does not exist (e.g. detail view for a deleted project); Project Management returns a not-found result; Web Ingress renders a 404 page
